1.tip: 30° incline. 3-second descent. Elbows at 45°.

2.tip: Heavier than Phase 1. Straight to A2.

3.tip: 30° incline. Control descent. Elbows 45°. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Heaviest pressing of your programme. Control descent fully. Strength benchmark.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: reduce incline toward flat, neutral grip, press only to a pain-free height. Lighter load.

1.tip: Handles at mid-chest. Full ROM.

3.tip: Full ROM, squeeze at peak. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Full ROM. Squeeze at peak contraction.

1.tip: Seated, neutral grip. Press straight up.

maintenance.tip: Seated, neutral grip. Heavy.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: swapped to Dumbbell Lateral Raise — no overhead pressing. Light dumbbells, elbow leads to shoulder height only, controlled descent.
shoulder.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise

1.tip: Single arm. Slight lean away. Elbow leads.

3.tip: Light weight. Lead with elbow. Rest 40s after A3.

maintenance.tip: Lighter. Shoulder health and definition.

1.tip: Elbows pinned to sides. Spread rope at bottom.

3.tip: Elbows pinned. Spread rope at bottom. Rest 40s after B3.

maintenance.tip: Elbows fixed. Full extension.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: light load, elbows pinned, partial ROM (no full lockout). Stop before pain.

1.tip: Hinge at elbow only. Long-head emphasis.

3.tip: Hinge at elbow only. Rest 40s after B3.

maintenance.tip: Hinge at elbow only. Full stretch.

elbow.name: Dumbbell Overhead Extension (Neutral)
elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: neutral-grip dumbbell overhead extension is gentler on the elbow than a straight-bar overhead.

shoulder.name: Skull Crusher
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: lying skull crusher — the arm stays in front of the body instead of overhead.

1.tip: Controlled, feel the chest stretch.

2.tip: Controlled, focus on chest squeeze. Straight to C2.

3.tip: Full stretch. Squeeze at peak. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Full ROM. Chest squeeze at peak.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: reduce the stretch range (stop before the shoulders feel pulled back), lighter load.

1.tip: Elbows track back, not flaring.

2.tip: Elbows track back. Rest 45s after C2.

3.tip: Elbows track back. Straight to B3.

maintenance.tip: Add weight on lap for progression.

elbow.name: Close-Grip Neutral Dumbbell Press
elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: neutral grip dumbbell press — no shoulder loading from dip position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the dip shallow (no deep shoulder extension), feet forward to offload. Stop at the first pinch.

1.tip: Arms straight. Pull to chest width. Squeeze shoulder blades.

2.tip: Arms straight. Pull to chest width. Rest 45s.

3.tip: Arms straight. Rotator cuff health. Rest 40s.

maintenance.tip: Daily shoulder health volume.

1.tip: Rope to forehead. External rotate at top — hands end behind ears.

2.tip: Rope to forehead, external rotate at top. Rest 45s.

3.tip: Rope to forehead. External rotate at top. Rest 40s after B3.

maintenance.tip: Mandatory for shoulder health. Rope to forehead. Hands end behind ears.

1.tip: Slight forward lean. Elbows back. Full depth.

2.tip: Add weight via belt. Straight to C2.

3.tip: Add weight via belt or dumbbell between legs. Elbows back.

maintenance.tip: Add weight via belt or dumbbell between legs. Elbows back.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: bodyweight only. No added load.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: bodyweight only, shallow depth, torso upright — never let the shoulders drop below the elbows.

1.tip: Kneeling or standing. Press at 45° angle. Great for shoulder health.

maintenance.tip: Heavy. Controlled arc. Great shoulder-safe pressing movement.

1.tip: High cables. Arms slightly bent. Squeeze at bottom. Full stretch at top.

2.tip: High cables. Squeeze at bottom. Straight to B2.

3.tip: High cables. Full stretch. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Full ROM. Squeeze at peak contraction.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: use low-to-high cable — arms stay below shoulder height.

1.tip: Pull to upper chest. Shoulder blades down first.

2.tip: Pull to upper chest, lean slightly back. Straight to A2.

3.tip: Shoulder blades first. Pull to upper chest. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Weighted pull-up preferred. Pull to upper chest. Full dead hang between reps.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: wider grip only. Pull to upper chest — no behind-neck.

1.tip: Chest proud. Hold contraction 1 second.

3.tip: Chest proud, hold 1s. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: 2-second hold at contraction. Mid-back focus.

1.tip: Brace on bench. Full ROM. Elbow past body.

2.tip: Full ROM. Elbow past body. Straight to C1.

3.tip: Brace on bench. Full ROM. Elbow past body. Straight to B1.

maintenance.tip: Heavy. Pull to hip, not ribcage. Elbow past body.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: brace one hand on a bench so the torso is fully supported; keep the spine neutral, light load.

1.tip: Rope to forehead. External rotate at top — hands end behind ears.

2.tip: External rotate at top. Rest 45s after C2.

3.tip: Rope to forehead. Hands end behind ears. Rest 40s after A3.

maintenance.tip: Mandatory for shoulder health in maintenance. External rotate at top.

1.tip: Elbows fixed. Full extension. 3-second lower.

2.tip: Elbows fixed. Full extension. Rest 45s after A2.

maintenance.tip: Heavy. Full extension. 3-second lower.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: neutral/hammer grip, lighter load, no full extension at the bottom. Stop before pain.

1.tip: Neutral grip. Targets brachialis. Control descent.

3.tip: Neutral grip. Targets brachialis. Rest 40s after B3.

maintenance.tip: Neutral grip. Heavy. Elbows fixed.

1.tip: Longer ROM. Great bicep stretch at bottom.

2.tip: Longer ROM. Great stretch. Straight to B2.

3.tip: Longer ROM. Great stretch. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Full stretch at bottom. Controlled throughout.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: neutral grip preferred. Longer ROM but stop before pain.

1.tip: Hip hinge, back flat. Pull to lower chest. Squeeze shoulder blades.

2.tip: Heavy horizontal pull. Elbow past body. Straight to A2.

3.tip: Pull to hip not ribcage. Elbow past body. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Heaviest horizontal pull. Pull to hip, not ribcage. Elbow past body.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: brace the chest on an incline bench (chest-supported) and use light dumbbells; keep the spine neutral.

1.tip: Dead hang start. Pull chin over bar. Full dead hang between reps.

2.tip: Add weight via belt. Full dead hang. Straight to A2.

3.tip: Add weight via belt. Full dead hang. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Add weight via belt. Full dead hang between reps. Best vertical pull strength movement.

shoulder.name: Lat Pulldown
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: lat pulldown with adjusted grip width.

1.tip: Arms straight throughout. Push bar to hips. Pure lat isolation.

2.tip: Arms straight. Push to hips. Straight to B2.

3.tip: Arms straight. Push to hips. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Pure lat engagement. Arms locked straight.

1.tip: Rear delt focus. Slight elbow bend. Full squeeze at peak.

2.tip: Rear delt squeeze. Straight to C2.

maintenance.tip: Full ROM. Rear delt and rotator cuff health.

1.tip: Hip hinge, bar stays close. Feel hamstring stretch. Stop before rounding.

2.tip: Hip hinge. Feel hamstring stretch. Straight to A1.

3.tip: Hip hinge. Feel hamstring stretch. Straight to A1.

maintenance.tip: 4-second lowering for maximum hamstring TUT. Hip hinge, not squat.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: shorten the range so the hamstrings never reach a deep stretch; light load only. A seated leg curl is a safe alternative.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: drop the load and shorten the range to a hip-hinge you can keep perfectly flat-backed; or substitute an upright goblet squat. Stop at the first sign of rounding.

1.tip: Bench at mid-scapula. Drive to full extension. Squeeze 1s. Chin tucked.

2.tip: Drive through heels. Straight to B1.

3.tip: Drive to full extension. Squeeze 1s. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Drive through heels. Full hip extension. 2-second hold at top.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: remove the bar (bodyweight only), engage the pelvic floor before each rep, and keep the range pain-free.

1.tip: Feet mid-plate. Controlled descent. Don't lock knees.

maintenance.tip: Heavy. Full ROM. Don't lock out.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: feet high on the platform, stop at ~90 degree knee bend (no deeper), reduce load. Do not chase depth.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the lower back flat on the pad through the whole range; do not let the pelvis tuck at the bottom.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: stop short of deep knee flexion to limit hamstring stretch; keep tension on the quads.

1.tip: Full ROM. 3-second controlled return.

3.tip: Full ROM. Controlled return. Rest 40s after A3.

maintenance.tip: Full ROM. 3-second lower.

1.tip: Cable between legs. Hinge at hip, drive forward.

2.tip: Hip hinge, drive forward. Rest 45s after C2.

3.tip: Hip hinge. Drive forward. Rest 40s after B2.

maintenance.tip: Heavy. Full hip extension. Good lower back alternative to RDL.

1.tip: Slow both ways. Critical for knee stability.

2.tip: Slow both ways. Rest 45s after A2.

3.tip: Slow both ways. No slamming. Rest 40s after B3.

maintenance.tip: Glute medius — critical for knee and hip health long-term.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: remain seated throughout. Slow and controlled.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: pure hip abduction places no load on the hamstrings; keep the movement slow and controlled.

1.tip: Bar on traps. Hinge at hips. Slight knee bend. Feel hamstring stretch. Back flat.

2.tip: Hinge at hips. Hamstring stretch. Straight to C1.

3.tip: Pause at stretch. Hinge at hips. Straight to C1.

maintenance.tip: Moderate weight. Full hamstring stretch. Back flat throughout.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: very light load, shallow hinge, ribs-down brace. If the lower back complains, switch to a machine leg press instead.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: avoid the deep lengthened position; keep the hinge shallow and light, or use a quad-dominant leg press instead.

1.tip: Step back. Front knee tracks over toes. Upright torso. Drive through front heel.

2.tip: Step back. Front heel drive. Straight to C2.

3.tip: Step back. Full depth. Straight to C2.

maintenance.tip: Weighted. Upright torso. Drive through front heel.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: shorter step. Do not flex front knee past 90°.

1.tip: Full stretch at bottom. Full contraction at top. 1s hold.

2.tip: Full stretch and contraction. Straight to C2.

3.tip: Full stretch. Full contraction. Straight to C2.

maintenance.tip: Slow and heavy. Full ROM.

ankle.name: Standing Calf Raise
ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: seated only. No standing loading on the ankle.

1.tip: Partner holds ankles. Lower as slowly as possible. Push up with hands. Elite hamstring builder.

3.tip: Maximum eccentric control. Straight to C1.

maintenance.tip: Max eccentric control. Lower over 5 seconds.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: do NOT perform the heavy Nordic eccentric on an injured hamstring — use band-assisted partial reps only, or substitute an unloaded glute bridge.

1.tip: Seated. Press straight up. Neutral grip option.

maintenance.tip: Heavy. Seated.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: swapped to Cable Face Pull — no overhead lockout. Light, pull to the forehead with elbows high, external-rotate at the end. Rotator-cuff friendly.

1.tip: Strict form. Shoulder height only.

maintenance.tip: Strict. No momentum.

1.tip: Elbows pinned. Full extension, spread rope.

2.tip: Elbows pinned. Full extension, spread rope.

maintenance.tip: Elbows pinned. Full extension.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: rope pushdown with light load, elbows pinned to the sides, no full lockout. Stop before pain.

maintenance.tip: Full rotation. Controlled descent.

shoulder.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: lateral raises only — no pressing overhead.

3.tip: Arms straight. Rear delt squeeze. Rotator cuff health.

maintenance.tip: Daily volume. Essential shoulder health in maintenance.

1.tip: Neutral grip option. Press straight up.

2.tip: Press straight up. Straight to A1.

3.tip: Press straight up. Straight to A1.

maintenance.tip: Heavy. Neutral grip preferred.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: swapped to Face Pull — no overhead lockout. Light, pull to the forehead with elbows high, external-rotate at the end. Rotator-cuff friendly.
shoulder.name: Cable Face Pull

1.tip: Single arm. Slight lean away. Elbow leads. Shoulder height only.

2.tip: Strict form. Shoulder height. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Strict form. No momentum.

2.tip: Rope to forehead. External rotate. Straight to B2.

3.tip: Rope to forehead. External rotate. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Mandatory. Rope to forehead. External rotate at top.

2.tip: Rear delt squeeze. Straight to C1.

maintenance.tip: Full ROM. Squeeze at peak. Rear delt health.

3.tip: Elbows flare. Stop at chin. Straight to C2.

maintenance.tip: Elbows lead. Stop at chin height.

3.tip: Alternating. Shoulder height. Straight to B1.

maintenance.tip: Alternating. Shoulder height only.

maintenance.tip: Heavy. 1-second hold at peak.

2.tip: Y angle strictly maintained. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Shoulder health. Y angle strictly maintained.

1.tip: Elbows fixed. Full extension at bottom.

maintenance.tip: Neutral grip. Hinge at elbow only. Long-head emphasis.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: pushdown only — overhead flexion loads an irritated shoulder.

maintenance.tip: Full overhead stretch. Long-head tricep emphasis.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: pushdown only — overhead extension stresses inflamed elbow.

shoulder.name: Resistance Band Tricep Pushdown
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: band pushdown — elbows pinned to the sides, nothing goes overhead.

2.tip: Palms facing each other. Rest 45s after C2.

maintenance.tip: Neutral grip throughout. No wrist loading.

1.tip: Lower bar to forehead. Elbows stay fixed pointing up. Full stretch.

2.tip: Lower to forehead. Elbows fixed. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Full stretch. No elbow flare.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: lighter load, lower to the forehead only (partial ROM), keep elbows from flaring. Stop before pain.

2.tip: Overhand grip. Elbows fixed. Straight to C2.

maintenance.tip: Strict overhand. Builds forearm thickness.

1.tip: Stand inside the bar. Hip-width feet. Neutral spine. Push floor away.

2.tip: Neutral spine. Push floor away. Straight to A2.

3.tip: Heavy. Neutral spine. Hips and shoulders rise together. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Heaviest full-body pull. Hips and shoulders rise together. 3-4 min rest.

lowerBack.name: Goblet Squat
lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: goblet squat — upright torso removes spinal compression.

knee.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: hip thrust is the primary compound movement. Full glute squeeze at top.

1.tip: Use leg drive to initiate. Explosive overhead press.

2.tip: Leg drive, explosive press. Straight to A2.

3.tip: Explosive. Leg drive. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Explosive. Power development.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: use leg drive but stay controlled — avoid explosive jump.

shoulder.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: no overhead pressing — lateral raise instead.

1.tip: Each side. Rotate through torso, not arms. Core anti-rotation strength.

2.tip: Rotate through torso. Straight to B2.

3.tip: Each side. Rotate through torso. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Each side. Torso rotation. Anti-rotation core work.

1.tip: Press lower back into floor throughout. Each side. Slow. Breathe out as you lower.

2.tip: Lower back pressed down. Each side. Slow and controlled.

3.tip: Lower back down. Each side. Core stability.

maintenance.tip: Press lower back into floor. Core stability work.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: engage pelvic floor and transverse abdominis before each rep. Press lower back into floor.

1.tip: Hip hinge, not a squat. Drive hips forward explosively. Bell reaches shoulder height.

2.tip: Explosive hip drive. Straight to B2.

3.tip: Explosive hip drive. Bell to shoulder height. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Heavy bell. Explosive. Full hip extension at top.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: do NOT perform ballistic swings with a back injury — substitute a slow cable pull-through or a dead-bug core hold instead.

1.tip: Front rack. Squat deep. Drive up and press overhead in one movement. Full body power.

2.tip: Squat and press. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Moderate weight. Power development.

shoulder.name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: goblet squat only — no overhead press. Dumbbell held at the chest.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: keep depth ABOVE parallel — stop well before the knee passes 90°. Reduce load; prioritise the press over the squat.

maintenance.tip: Heaviest pressing of your programme. Control descent fully. Strength benchmark. Barbell or heavy dumbbells.

shoulder.name: Machine Chest Press
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: machine press. Full ROM, squeeze at peak.

maintenance.tip: Full ROM. Elbows 45° from torso.
Seated, neutral grip. Heavy.

shoulder.name: Cable Lateral Raise
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: strict lateral raise only. Shoulder height.

maintenance.tip: Add weight via belt or dumbbell between legs. Elbows back.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: bodyweight only. No added load.

shoulder.name: Tricep Rope Pushdown
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: rope pushdown instead — elbow-only hinge, no shoulder loading or impingement.

maintenance.tip: Add weight via belt. Full dead hang between reps. Best vertical pull strength movement.

shoulder.name: Lat Pulldown
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: lat pulldown with adjusted grip.

maintenance.tip: Heaviest horizontal pull. Pull to hip, not ribcage. Elbow past body. Barbell or heavy dumbbells.

lowerBack.name: Chest-Supported Dumbbell Row
lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: chest-supported row.

maintenance.tip: 2-second hold at contraction. Mid-back focus.
Heavy. Full extension. 3-second lower.

elbow.name: Hammer Curl
elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: neutral grip. Heavy. Elbows fixed.

maintenance.tip: Mandatory for shoulder health in maintenance. External rotate at top.
Primary strength movement. Hips and shoulders rise together. Neutral spine. 4-5 min rest.

knee.name: Leg Press (Partial ROM)
knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: heavy leg press is the primary compound movement — feet high on the plate, 60° max depth so the knee never passes the toes. Drive through heels.

lowerBack.name: Goblet Squat
lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: heavy goblet squat. Upright torso.

maintenance.tip: Drive through heels. Full hip extension. 2-second hold at top.

hip.name: Floor Glute Bridge (Unloaded)
hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: bodyweight only — no load across the hips (avoids intra-abdominal pressure). Engage pelvic floor before each rep. Full extension.

maintenance.tip: 4-second lowering for maximum hamstring TUT. Hip hinge, not squat.

hamstring.name: Seated Leg Curl (Light)
hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: light resistance only. Stay in a pain-free range. Stop at discomfort — no aggressive loading.

lowerBack.name: Cable Pull-Through
lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: cable pull-through.

maintenance.tip: Full ROM. 3-second lower.
Glute medius — critical for maintaining knee and hip health long-term.
Primary push strength. Heavy.
Primary pull strength. Add weight via belt.

shoulder.name: Chest-Supported Dumbbell Row
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: chest-supported row — no loaded overhead hang, torso carried by the bench.

maintenance.tip: Heavy horizontal pull. Back flat.
Your heaviest home press. Feet on a chair or sofa, 3-second descent with a pause at the bottom. Raise the feet higher as it gets easy. 3 min rest.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: lower the feet to the floor (standard push-up) or go to an incline; overhead-leaning load can aggravate the shoulder.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth, hands slightly wider.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: brace hard so the hips do not sag; regress to a flat push-up.

maintenance.tip: Hands in a diamond under the chest. Triceps strength work. Slow descent, elbows tight to the ribs.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: widen the hands to a standard push-up to reduce elbow load.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep it shallow, elbows pinned to the ribs.

maintenance.tip: Your vertical press. Hips high, head between the hands. Elevate the feet on a step to load it more.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: reduce the range and keep the hips a little lower (more incline); stop before any overhead pinch. Regress to the wall pike.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth and keep the elbows tracking back, not flaring.

maintenance.tip: 5-6 reps EACH side. The closest thing to a heavy single-arm press at home. If you cannot control the descent, do the Wide Push-Up in this session instead and build up to it.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: skip the deep unilateral reach - do a standard or incline push-up to a pain-free depth instead.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: shorten the range and keep the straight arm softly bent, never locked.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: brace the abs and squeeze the glutes so the hips do not sag; regress to a wide push-up.

maintenance.tip: Chest-focused finisher and the beginner-friendly option in this session. Slow tempo, pause at the bottom.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: bring the hands in narrower and reduce depth; a very wide grip stresses the shoulder.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth.

maintenance.tip: Your heaviest home pull. Under a sturdy table or between two chairs with a broom handle. Straighten the legs (heels only) to make it harder. 3 min rest.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: use a neutral (palms-facing) grip and stop the pull before any shoulder pinch.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: bend the knees and plant the feet flat so the trunk stays supported; keep a neutral spine.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: use a neutral grip and avoid fully locking the elbows at the bottom.

maintenance.tip: Underhand grip - this is your biceps strength work. Pull the chest to the edge, 2s squeeze, 3-second lower.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: avoid fully locking out at the bottom and keep the load light.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: stop the pull before any shoulder pinch.

maintenance.tip: Posterior chain. Lift the chest and thighs together, 2-second hold at the top of every rep.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: lift the opposite arm and leg only (bird-dog style) with a small range; skip if extension is painful.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: keep the leg lift small and controlled.

maintenance.tip: Face down, thumbs up, arms overhead into a Y. Lower traps. Light and strict - quality over reps.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: raise only within a pain-free range and keep the movement light.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: rest the forehead down and keep the chest low so the lower back stays neutral.

maintenance.tip: Non-negotiable shoulder health work in maintenance - the home stand-in for face pulls. Slow sweeps, arms hovering the whole set.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: reduce the overhead range; stop where the shoulder feels the pinch.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the chest low and ribs down.

maintenance.tip: 6-8 reps EACH leg. Your primary lower-body strength movement at home. Rear foot on a chair, 3-second descent, pause at the bottom. Hold a bag or bottles to progress. If balance is the limit, do the Reverse Lunge in this session instead.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: reduce the depth and keep the front knee tracking the toes; regress to a standing split squat.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: lower the rear-foot elevation and control the range.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: keep the front foot flat and shorten the range.

maintenance.tip: 8 reps EACH leg. Hamstring hinge - the home replacement for the Romanian deadlift. Slow hinge, pause at the stretch, soft knee. Hold a weight to progress.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: reduce the range so the stretch is mild, keep a soft knee; stop before any sharp pull.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the spine neutral, use the kickstand (back toes down) regression and a short range.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: use the kickstand version and hold a wall for balance.

maintenance.tip: 10 reps EACH side. Glute strength - the home hip thrust. Drive through the heel, 2-second hold at full hip extension.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: use both feet (double-leg bridge) and engage the pelvic floor before each rep; no added load across the hips.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: bring the heel closer to the hips so the glute does the work, not the hamstring; stop before any cramp or pull.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: ribs down, do not over-arch at the top - stop the lift level with the thigh.

maintenance.tip: 8-10 reps EACH leg. Quad and glute volume, and the knee-friendlier option in this session. Slow descent, pause at the bottom.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: shorten the range and keep the front knee behind the toes; reverse lunges are gentler than forward.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: keep the step-back short and controlled.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: reduce the depth and keep the front foot flat.

maintenance.tip: 15-20 reps EACH side. Glute medius - the home stand-in for the abduction machine, and what keeps the knees and hips healthy long term. Slow and strict; add a band if you have one.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: keep the opening small and pain-free; stop if anything pinches at the front of the hip.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the pelvis stacked and still - the trunk should not roll back with the knee.

maintenance.tip: Main press of the session. 3-second descent, 1-second pause at the bottom. Elevate the feet or slow it further as it gets easy.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: elevate the hands (incline push-up), elbows ~45 degrees.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth, hands slightly wider.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: brace the abs and squeeze the glutes; drop to knees if the back sags.

maintenance.tip: Main pull of the session. Grip a solid door frame, lean back and row the chest to the hands. Step the feet forward to increase the load. 2s squeeze at the top.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the elbows low and stop before any shoulder pinch.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: avoid fully locking the elbows at the stretch.

maintenance.tip: Overhead press pattern. Feet walked up a wall, hips stacked over the shoulders. Slow tempo with a pause; walk the feet higher to progress.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the feet low and the range shallow; move only pain-free.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: shorten the range and keep the elbows tracking back, not flaring.

maintenance.tip: Triceps strength against a table edge or a solid worktop. Elbows stay high and still; step the feet back to increase the load.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: stand more upright to reduce the load and keep the range short of pain.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the upper arms lower and the range comfortable.

maintenance.tip: Shoulder health work to finish - keeps the overhead pressing pain-free long term. Slow slides, pause at the top with the arms tall, ribs down.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: slide only as high as stays pain-free; keep the ribs down.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the lower back flat against the wall throughout - do not let it arch to gain height.

1.tip: Full depth. Controlled descent. Core tight.

maintenance.tip: Add weight plate on back for progression.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: elevate the hands on a bench/counter (incline) to reduce shoulder load and shorten the painful arc.

1.tip: Elbows stop at floor. Full press. ROM limited by floor.

2.tip: Elbows stop at floor. Straight to A1.

maintenance.tip: Heavy dumbbells. Full press from floor.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep elbows tucked ~45 degrees and press only to a pain-free height. Lighter dumbbells.

1.tip: Hips high. Lower head toward floor. Shoulder emphasis.

2.tip: Hips high. Head to floor. Straight to A2.

3.tip: Hips high. Slow descent. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Hips high throughout. Head to floor.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: reduce the range and keep the hips a little lower (more incline); stop before any overhead pinch. Regress to the wall pike.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth and keep the elbows tracking back, not flaring.

1.tip: Seated on floor or chair. Strict form. Press straight up.

2.tip: Seated. Press straight up. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Heavy. Seated.

shoulder.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: no overhead pressing — lead with the elbow to shoulder height only.

1.tip: Hands form diamond under chest. Elbows tight to body.

3.tip: Elbows tight. Slow. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Elbows tight throughout. Tricep emphasis.

1.tip: Dead hang start. Pull chin over bar. Full dead hang between reps.

2.tip: Dead hang. Pull chin over. Straight to A1.

3.tip: Dead hang. Full ROM. Straight to A1.

maintenance.tip: Add weight for progression. Full dead hang.

shoulder.name: Doorway Row
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: horizontal pull instead of a loaded overhead hang. Feet forward, chest to hands, elbows close.

1.tip: Brace on chair. Full ROM. Elbow past body.

2.tip: Elbow past body. Straight to A2.

3.tip: Full ROM. Elbow past body. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Heavy. Full ROM. Elbow past body.

1.tip: Bent over. Elbows wide. Squeeze rear delts.

3.tip: Rear delt squeeze. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Bent over. Full ROM. Rear delt health.

1.tip: Full extension at bottom. Elbows fixed.

2.tip: Full extension. Elbows fixed. Straight to C1.

3.tip: Full extension. Elbows fixed. Straight to C1.

maintenance.tip: Full extension. Strict form.

elbow.name: Hammer Curl
elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: neutral grip throughout.

1.tip: Arms straight. Pull to chest width. Squeeze shoulder blades.

2.tip: Arms straight. Shoulder blade squeeze. Straight to C2.

3.tip: Arms straight. Pull to chest. Straight to C2.

maintenance.tip: Daily shoulder health. Arms locked straight.

1.tip: Hip hinge. Feel hamstring stretch at bottom.

2.tip: Hip hinge. Hamstring stretch. Straight to A1.

3.tip: Hip hinge. Full stretch. Straight to A1.

maintenance.tip: Heavy dumbbells. 4-second lower. Full stretch.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: avoid the loaded hinge/stretch — substitute a clamshell or glute bridge (hip work without hamstring lengthening).

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: drop the dumbbells and shorten the hinge, or substitute a single-leg glute bridge — keep the spine neutral throughout.

1.tip: Shoulders on bench/sofa. Drive through heels. Full extension. Squeeze glutes.

3.tip: Full extension. 2s hold. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Add dumbbell or plate for progression.

hip.name: Floor Glute Bridge (Unloaded)
hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: engage pelvic floor before each rep. Bodyweight only.

1.tip: dumbbell at chest. Upright torso. Depth to parallel. Elbows inside knees.

2.tip: Upright torso. Depth to parallel. Straight to B1.

3.tip: Pause 1s at depth. Upright torso. Straight to B1.

maintenance.tip: Heavy dumbbell. Pause at depth.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: heels elevated on a plate or book. Depth reduced — stop before discomfort.

1.tip: Step back. Front knee tracks over toes. Drive through front heel.

2.tip: Step back. Front heel drive. Straight to C2.

3.tip: Step back. Full depth. Straight to C2.

maintenance.tip: Add dumbbells for progression. Upright torso.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: shorter step. Front knee behind toes strictly.

ankle.name: Seated Dumbbell Calf Press
ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: seated calf press only. Stop if painful.

1.tip: Side-lying. Feet together. Open top knee. Glute medius activation.

2.tip: Slow and controlled. Straight to B2.

3.tip: Glute medius. Slow. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Add band for resistance. Slow and controlled.

1.tip: One leg extended. Drive hips up. 1s hold at top. Both sides.

2.tip: Drive hips up. 1s hold. Straight to B2.

3.tip: Slow descent. 1s hold. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Add weight on hips for progression.

1.tip: One arm straight, one arm bends to lower. Advanced push-up variation. Builds single-arm strength.

2.tip: One arm bends. Straight to A2.

3.tip: Slow and controlled. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Single-arm strength focus. Both sides.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: skip the deep unilateral reach — do a standard or incline push-up to a pain-free depth, or a floor press instead.

1.tip: Grip table edge. Body straight. Pull chest to table. Feet further = harder.

2.tip: Pull chest to table. Hold 1s. Straight to A2.

3.tip: Pull to table. Hold. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Feet elevated for progression. Full ROM.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep body in neutral spine. Don't arch.

1.tip: Controlled form.

maintenance.tip: Strength focus.

shoulder.name: Machine Chest Press
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: machine press removes impingement risk. Full ROM.

1.tip: Controlled form.

3.tip: Chest proud, hold 1s. Straight to A3.

maintenance.tip: Strength focus.

lowerBack.name: Chest-Supported Dumbbell Row
lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: chest-supported removes spinal loading.

1.tip: Controlled form.

2.tip: Pull to upper chest. Straight to B2.

3.tip: Shoulder blades first. Rest 40s after A3.

maintenance.tip: Strength focus.

shoulder.name: Lat Pulldown (Wide Grip)
shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: wider grip, pull to upper chest only.

1.tip: Controlled form.

maintenance.tip: Strength focus.

elbow.name: Hammer Curl
elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: hammer curl — neutral grip reduces strain on the elbow tendons vs. the EZ bar.

1.tip: Low pulleys. Drive up to shoulder height. Squeeze upper chest.

2.tip: Low-to-high path. Squeeze at top. Straight to C2.

3.tip: Low-to-high. Upper chest focus. Straight to C2.

maintenance.tip: Heavier. Controlled low-to-high path. 1s squeeze.

1.tip: Chest on an incline bench. Row dumbbells to ribs. No spinal load.

2.tip: Chest supported. Elbows past body. Straight to A2.

3.tip: Chest supported. Full ROM. Straight to A2.

maintenance.tip: Heavy chest-supported row. 1s squeeze at top.

1.tip: Slight elbow bend. Raise to shoulder height only. Control descent.

2.tip: Shoulder height strictly. Straight to B2.

3.tip: Light weight. Shoulder height. Straight to B2.

maintenance.tip: Strict form. Shoulder height. Definition and health.

1.tip: Lie flat, feet planted. Drive hips up via glutes. No added load.

2.tip: Bodyweight only. 1s squeeze at top. Straight to B3.

3.tip: Unloaded. Full hip extension. Straight to B3.

maintenance.tip: Bodyweight activation. Pause 1s at top each rep.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: drive through the heels and glutes, not the hamstrings; reduce range if the hamstrings engage.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: engage the pelvic floor before each rep and keep the range pain-free; bodyweight only.

1.tip: Hold dumbbell/kettlebell at chest. Upright torso. Squat to depth. Drive through heels.

2.tip: Front-loaded. Upright torso. Straight to A3.

3.tip: Upright torso protects the spine. Straight to A3.

maintenance.tip: Heavy goblet hold. Upright torso. Full depth.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the torso tall and brace the core; reduce depth if the lower back rounds.

1.tip: Hands elevated on a bench/counter. Body straight. Controlled descent.

2.tip: Hands elevated. Full ROM. Straight to A3.

3.tip: Hands elevated. Reduced shoulder load. Straight to A3.

maintenance.tip: Lower the surface for more load. Body straight. Full ROM.

1.tip: Machine-guided. Stop at ~90 degree knee — no deeper. Protects the knee.

2.tip: Partial ROM. 90 degree knee max. Straight to C3.

3.tip: Partial ROM. No deep flexion. Straight to C3.

maintenance.tip: Partial ROM. Controlled. 90 degree knee limit.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: keep the range above 90 degrees and the feet high on the platform to reduce patellar load.

1.tip: Seated, dumbbell on knees. Press through balls of feet. Controlled range.

2.tip: Seated calf press. Full controlled ROM. Straight to C3.

3.tip: Seated. Pause at top. Straight to C3.

maintenance.tip: Seated calf. Heavier dumbbells. 1s squeeze at top.

1.tip: Light resistance. Controlled curl. No ballistic motion — hamstring-safe.

2.tip: Light load. Slow tempo. Straight to A3.

3.tip: Light. Pain-free range only. Straight to A3.

maintenance.tip: Light resistance. Strict controlled curl.

1.tip: On forearms and toes, body in one straight line — squeeze glutes and brace your abs. Don't let the hips sag or pike. Beginners: drop to the knees and hold.

maintenance.tip: Longer holds. Neutral spine, ribs down.

1.tip: On one forearm, body straight, hips stacked and lifted. Don't let the top hip roll forward. Beginners: bend the bottom knee for support. Switch sides.

maintenance.tip: Hold each side. Lift the hips high.

1.tip: Lie on your back, arms up, knees at 90. Slowly lower the opposite arm and leg while pressing your low back FLAT into the floor. If it arches, shorten the range.

maintenance.tip: Slow and controlled. Low back stays pinned.

1.tip: On hands and knees, extend the opposite arm and leg to a straight line without twisting the hips. Pause, then return under control. Keep the spine neutral throughout.

maintenance.tip: Slow reps, brief hold at the top.

1.tip: Bridge your hips up and hold that height, then march one knee up at a time without letting the hips drop or tilt. Squeeze the glutes the whole set.

maintenance.tip: Keep the hips level as you march.

1.tip: Lie on your back, press your low back into the floor, then lift the shoulders and legs into a shallow banana shape. Lower the legs if the back lifts. Beginners: tuck the knees.

maintenance.tip: Longer holds. Low back stays glued down.

1.tip: Bring the opposite elbow toward the raised knee, extending the other leg. Rotate through the trunk, not the neck. Move with control — don't yank on your head.

maintenance.tip: Steady tempo, full rotation.

1.tip: Lie down, knees bent, and curl the knees toward your chest by lifting the hips off the floor — then lower slowly. Use the abs, not momentum. Keep the movement small and controlled.

maintenance.tip: Controlled lift, slow lower.

1.tip: Flat on your back, legs straight, hands under your hips. Lower the legs slowly and only as far as you can keep the low back pressed down — then raise. Bend the knees to make it easier.

maintenance.tip: Slow lower. Stop if the back arches.

1.tip: From a high plank with feet wide, tap the opposite shoulder with each hand while keeping the hips DEAD still — no rocking side to side. Slower is harder and better.

maintenance.tip: Minimise hip sway. Brace hard.

1.tip: Stand side-on to a cable or anchored band at chest height. Press the handle straight out and resist it pulling you into rotation — brace hard — then return. Switch sides.

maintenance.tip: Anti-rotation. Press slow, resist the twist.

1.tip: Hang from a bar, shoulders active (not fully slack). Curl the knees up toward the chest by rounding the pelvis — don't just swing the hips. Lower under control; no kipping.

maintenance.tip: Controlled. Stop the swing.

1.tip: Kneel at a high cable, rope at the sides of your head. Crunch by curling the ribs toward the pelvis — flex the SPINE, don't just fold at the hips. Slow return.

maintenance.tip: Round the spine, resist on the way up.

1.tip: ADVANCED — roll the wheel out only as far as you can keep a braced, NON-arching low back, then pull back with the abs. BEGINNERS: do this from the knees with a short range, or hold a plank instead until you build the strength.

maintenance.tip: Short, braced range. Never let the back sag.

1.tip: Hands under shoulders, body in one line. Full range, chest near floor.

maintenance.tip: Add a 2s pause at the bottom. Slow, strict reps.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: elevate the hands on a bench/wall (incline push-up) and keep elbows ~45 degrees to unload the shoulder.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth and keep hands a touch wider so the elbows travel less.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: brace the abs and squeeze the glutes so the hips do not sag; drop to knees if the back rounds.

1.tip: Regression for the push-up. Knees down, hips in line with shoulders, full chest range.

maintenance.tip: Slow tempo, pause at the bottom. Progress toward full push-ups.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the range shallow and elbows tucked; stop before any shoulder pinch.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth, hands slightly wider.

1.tip: Feet elevated on a chair/step to load the upper chest. Body straight, controlled descent.

maintenance.tip: Slow descent with a pause. Higher foot elevation increases the challenge.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: lower the feet to the floor (standard push-up) or go to an incline; overhead-leaning load can aggravate the shoulder.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth, hands slightly wider.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: brace hard so the hips do not sag; regress to a flat push-up.

1.tip: Hands wider than shoulders to bias the chest. Elbows flare slightly, full range.

maintenance.tip: Slow tempo, pause at the bottom.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: bring the hands in narrower and reduce depth; a very wide grip stresses the shoulder.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth.

1.tip: Hands together forming a diamond under the chest to bias the triceps. Elbows tuck to the ribs.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled reps, pause at the bottom.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: widen the hands to a standard push-up to reduce elbow load.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep it shallow, elbows pinned to the ribs.

1.tip: Chair or bench. Elbows track straight back. Controlled descent, do not shrug.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: feet on the ground with knees bent and keep the dip shallow — minimal shoulder loading.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: shallow depth, elbows pinned, light load. Stop before pain.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: press on the floor (floor press) so the elbows stop at the floor — limits the painful shoulder range. Neutral grip, lighter dumbbells.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: neutral grip, stop short of full elbow lockout, lighter load.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: reduce the incline toward flat, neutral grip, press to a pain-free height only.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: lighter weight, neutral grip, stop before pain.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: stagger your stance, lighter band, press to mid-chest only.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: lighter band, stop short of full lockout.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.
Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the arms near shoulder height with a small range — do not let the shoulders stretch back. Light dumbbells.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: keep the elbows bent ~90 degrees, avoid full extension.

1.tip: Lie under a sturdy table, grip the edge, body straight, pull the chest to the edge. Bend knees to make it easier.

maintenance.tip: Slower pull, 2s squeeze at the top. Straighten the legs to progress.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: use a neutral (palms-facing) grip and stop the pull before any shoulder pinch.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: bend the knees and plant the feet flat so the trunk stays supported; keep a neutral spine.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: use a neutral grip and avoid fully locking the elbows at the bottom.

1.tip: Face down, lift the chest, arms and legs a few inches. Squeeze the back, do not crank the neck.

maintenance.tip: Add a 2s hold at the top of each lift.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: lift the opposite arm and leg only (bird-dog style) with a small range; skip if extension is painful.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: keep the leg lift small and controlled.

1.tip: Face down, thumbs up, raise the arms overhead into a Y. Targets the lower traps. Light and strict.

maintenance.tip: Slow lift with a 2s hold. Keep the neck relaxed.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: raise only within a pain-free range and keep the movement light.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: rest the forehead down and keep the chest low so the lower back stays neutral.

1.tip: Face down, arms out to the sides into a T, thumbs up. Squeeze the shoulder blades. Rear delts and mid traps.

maintenance.tip: Slow lift with a 2s squeeze.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the arms slightly lower than shoulder height and move only pain-free.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the chest down; move only the arms.

1.tip: Face down, sweep the arms from hips to overhead and back, hovering off the floor. Scapular control.

maintenance.tip: Slow sweeps, pause overhead. Keep the arms hovering the whole set.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: reduce the overhead range; stop where the shoulder feels the pinch.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the chest low and ribs down.

1.tip: Grip both sides of a sturdy doorframe at chest height, feet close to the base, lean back with straight arms and pull yourself upright. Step the feet closer to make it easier.

maintenance.tip: Slower pull, 2s squeeze at the top. Step the feet forward to increase the load.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the elbows low and stop before any shoulder pinch.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: avoid fully locking the elbows at the stretch.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: use a thicker band for control and pull to chest height only — no overhead loading.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: kneel tall or sit and support the chest; keep the spine neutral with no hip hinge.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: stay upright with no hip hinge and brace the core throughout.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: use a light/thin band, keep the elbows pinned, and stop short of full extension.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: use a band to shorten the eccentric range; start from a 90 degree arm bend, not a full dead hang.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: use a thicker band for more support and a shorter range — do not drop into a full dead hang.

1.tip: Feet shoulder-width, sit back and down to at least parallel, drive through the heels. Chest tall.

maintenance.tip: Slow 3s descent with a pause at the bottom. Add a pause or slow tempo for more challenge.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: reduce the depth to a comfortable range and keep the knees tracking over the toes.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: narrow the stance and control the depth to a pain-free range.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: elevate the heels slightly on a book/plate and keep the range comfortable.

1.tip: Step back into a lunge, front shin vertical, back knee toward the floor. Push through the front heel. Alternate legs.

maintenance.tip: Slower descent, pause at the bottom. Hold weight for more load.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: shorten the range and keep the front knee behind the toes; reverse lunges are gentler than forward.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: keep the step-back short and controlled.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: reduce the depth and keep the front foot flat.

1.tip: Feet split front-to-back, lower straight down, front heel driving up. Torso tall.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: front knee stays at 90 degrees max — do not go deeper. Shorter range, reduce load.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: engage the pelvic floor before each rep and stay in a pain-free range.

1.tip: On your back, feet flat, drive the hips up and squeeze the glutes at the top. Ribs down.

maintenance.tip: Add a 2-3s squeeze at the top. Single-leg to progress.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: keep the feet a little closer so the glutes do the work, not the hamstrings; stop if the hamstring cramps.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: only bridge to a neutral line, do not over-arch; brace the abs at the top.

1.tip: Rise onto the balls of the feet through a full range, pause at the top, lower slowly. Hold a wall for balance.

maintenance.tip: Slow 3s lower and a 2s hold at the top. Single-leg to progress.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: reduce the range to pain-free and hold support; avoid a deep stretch at the bottom.

1.tip: Rear foot on a chair/bench, lower the back knee toward the floor, front shin near vertical. Regression: the standing split squat in this session.

maintenance.tip: Slower descent, pause at the bottom. Add weight to progress.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: reduce the depth and keep the front knee tracking the toes; regress to a standing split squat.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: lower the rear-foot elevation and control the range.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: keep the front foot flat and shorten the range.

1.tip: Hinge at the hip on one leg, back flat, free leg extending behind, feel the hamstring stretch, return tall. Advanced balance move. Regression: lightly rest the back toes down (kickstand RDL).

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled hinge with a pause at the stretch. Hold a weight to progress.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: reduce the range so the stretch is mild, keep a soft knee; stop before any sharp pull.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the spine neutral, use the kickstand (back toes down) regression and a short range.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: use the kickstand version and hold a wall for balance.

1.tip: On your back, opposite arm and leg extend while the low back stays pinned to the floor. Core and hip control.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: engage the pelvic floor before each rep and keep the range small — knees at 60 degrees max.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: press the lower back into the floor throughout — never let it lift.

1.tip: Dumbbells in front of the thighs, hinge at the hips with a flat back, slide the weights down the legs, feel the hamstrings, stand tall.

maintenance.tip: Heaviest controlled load, flat back, pause at the stretch.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: reduce the range so the stretch is mild and keep a soft knee.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the spine neutral, lighten the load and shorten the range; brace the abs.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: use a lower step (8-10 inches); the front knee stays at 90 degrees or less.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: stay controlled on the step-up — no explosive jumping.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: light dumbbells only and maintain a neutral spine throughout.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.
Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: squat to ~60 degrees only — stop well before parallel. Light band.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: light band only and engage the pelvic floor throughout.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: stay more upright (shallower squat) and use a light band only.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: reduce the step distance, stay controlled, light band only.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: place the band around the knees (not the ankles) and reduce the distance covered.

1.tip: Hips high in an inverted V, lower the crown of the head toward the floor, press back up. Bodyweight overhead press. Regression: the wall pike push-up in this session.

maintenance.tip: Slower descent with a pause. Elevate the feet to progress.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: reduce the range and keep the hips a little lower (more incline); stop before any overhead pinch. Regress to the wall pike.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth and keep the elbows tracking back, not flaring.

1.tip: Hands on the floor, feet walked up a wall (or on a low step) for an easier overhead angle. Lower with control, press up.

maintenance.tip: Slow tempo with a pause; walk the feet higher to progress.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the feet low and the range shallow; move only pain-free.

1.tip: Face down, thumbs up, raise the arms overhead into a Y. Lower traps and rear delts. Light and strict.

maintenance.tip: Slow lift with a 2s hold.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: raise only within a pain-free range and keep it light.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: rest the forehead down and keep the chest low.

1.tip: Face down, arms out into a T, thumbs up, squeeze the shoulder blades. Rear delts and mid traps.

maintenance.tip: Slow lift with a 2s squeeze.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the arms a touch below shoulder height and move pain-free.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the chest down; move only the arms.

1.tip: Face down, sweep the arms from the hips to overhead and back, hovering off the floor. Scapular control.

maintenance.tip: Slow sweeps with a pause overhead.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: reduce the overhead range; stop where the shoulder pinches.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the chest low and ribs down.

1.tip: Back and arms against a wall, slide the arms up and down keeping the wrists and elbows in contact. Shoulder mobility and control.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled slides, pause at the top with the arms tall.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: slide only as high as stays pain-free; keep the ribs down.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: reduce the incline toward flat, neutral grip, press only to a pain-free height.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: swapped to Dumbbell Lateral Raise — no overhead pressing. Lighter dumbbells, raise strictly to shoulder height, stop at the first sign of discomfort.
shoulder.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: lighter dumbbells and hinge deeper (closer to 90 degrees) to reduce shoulder load.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the hinge shallow (30 degrees max) and brace the core throughout.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: lighter dumbbells and raise to ear height ONLY — no higher, to prevent impingement.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: raise to shoulder height ONLY — stop before the impingement zone. Lighter band.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: lighter band, reduce the external-rotation range if painful, focus on scapular retraction.

1.tip: Hands about shoulder-width, elbows tucked to the ribs, lower under control. Triceps focus.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: elevate the hands on a bench (incline) to reduce load on an inflamed elbow.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: incline variation (hands elevated) reduces shoulder stress.

1.tip: Hands together in a diamond under the chest, elbows tuck tight. Strong triceps bias. Regression: the close-grip push-up in this session.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled reps with a pause at the bottom.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: widen the hands to a close-grip push-up to ease the elbow load.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep it shallow with the elbows pinned to the ribs.

1.tip: Hands on a chair edge, lower the hips straight down with the elbows tracking back. Bend the knees to regress.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: shallow depth, elbows pinned, light load — or do a standing band extension instead.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the dip shallow and feet on the floor; a band pushdown avoids dip loading.

1.tip: Hands on a sturdy waist-height surface, body angled forward, bend only at the elbows to lower the forehead, then extend. Bodyweight skull-crusher.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled reps; step the feet back to increase the load.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: stand more upright to reduce the load and keep the range short of pain.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the upper arms lower and the range comfortable.

1.tip: Under a sturdy table with an underhand (palms-up) grip, pull the chest up leading with the biceps. Bend the knees to regress.

maintenance.tip: Slower pull with a 2s squeeze; straighten the legs to progress.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: avoid fully locking out at the bottom and keep the load light.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: stop the pull before any shoulder pinch.

1.tip: Palm up, press your forearm up hard against your other hand (or a fixed surface) at a 90-degree elbow and hold for 10s. Self-resisted biceps.

maintenance.tip: Maximal-effort holds of 10-12s, full biceps tension, breathe.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: use a sub-maximal effort and a slightly more open elbow angle.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: light load, elbows fixed, stop short of full extension. A light band curl is a safe alternative.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: lighter dumbbell, slow controlled reps, stop short of full extension at the bottom.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: use a light/thin band only; full extension stops before pain.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: light band only, elbows pinned, avoid a high-load pushdown. Stop at comfort.

1.tip: Hands under shoulders, body in one line, chest near the floor. Drop to knees to regress.

maintenance.tip: Add a 2s pause at the bottom.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: elevate the hands (incline push-up), elbows ~45 degrees.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth, hands slightly wider.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: brace the abs and squeeze the glutes; drop to knees if the back sags.

1.tip: Hips high in an inverted V, lower the head toward the floor, press up. Shoulder-focused. Regression: keep the hips lower or use a wall.

maintenance.tip: Slow descent with a pause.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the hips lower (more incline) and the range shallow; stop before overhead pinch.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth.

1.tip: Under a sturdy table, body straight, pull the chest to the edge. Bend the knees to regress.

maintenance.tip: Slower pull with a 2s squeeze at the top.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: use a neutral grip and stop before any shoulder pinch.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: bend the knees, feet flat, keep a neutral spine.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: neutral grip, avoid locking out.

1.tip: Face down, lift the chest, arms and legs a few inches, squeeze the back. Do not crank the neck.

maintenance.tip: Add a 2s hold at the top.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: lift the opposite arm and leg only with a small range; skip if extension hurts.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: keep the leg lift small.

1.tip: Hands together in a diamond under the chest, elbows tuck. Triceps bias. Widen the hands to regress.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled reps.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: widen the hands to a standard push-up.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep it shallow, elbows pinned.

1.tip: Hands on a chair edge, lower the hips straight down, elbows track back. Bend the knees to regress.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: shallow depth, elbows pinned, light load — or do a band pressdown instead.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: feet on the floor and keep the dip shallow; stop before the shoulders complain.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep elbows tucked ~45 degrees and press only to a pain-free height. Lighter dumbbells.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: neutral grip, hinge at the elbow, stop before pain.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: switch to a single-arm row braced on a bench so the torso is supported; light load, neutral spine.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: swapped to Band Lateral Raise — upright rows pinch the shoulder. Raise strictly to shoulder height, lighter band, stop at the first sign of discomfort.
shoulder.name: Band Lateral Raise

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: lighter weight, raise strictly to shoulder height, stop at the first sign of discomfort.

maintenance.tip: Slow controlled tempo, full range. Add a pause in the hardest position.

1.tip: Stand on the band, press the handles overhead, lower under control. Ribs down, core braced.

maintenance.tip: Slower press with a pause at the top.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: press only to a pain-free height (short of full overhead) and keep the ribs down.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: brace the abs so the lower back does not arch; press to a comfortable height.

1.tip: Feet shoulder-width, sit down to parallel, drive through the heels, chest tall.

maintenance.tip: Slow 3s descent with a pause.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: reduce depth, knees tracking the toes.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: narrow the stance, control the depth.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: elevate the heels slightly and keep it comfortable.

1.tip: Hands under shoulders, body in one line, chest near the floor. Knees down to regress.

maintenance.tip: Add a 2s pause at the bottom.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: elevate the hands (incline push-up), elbows ~45 degrees.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth, hands slightly wider.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: brace the abs and glutes so the hips do not sag.

1.tip: Under a sturdy table, body straight, pull the chest to the edge. Bend the knees to regress.

maintenance.tip: Slower pull with a 2s squeeze.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: neutral grip, stop before any shoulder pinch.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: bend the knees, feet flat, neutral spine.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: neutral grip, avoid locking out.

1.tip: Step back into a lunge, front shin vertical, push through the front heel. Alternate legs.

maintenance.tip: Slower descent with a pause; hold weight to progress.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: shorten the range, front knee behind the toes.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: keep the step-back short and controlled.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: reduce depth, front foot flat.

1.tip: On your back, feet flat, drive the hips up, squeeze the glutes. Ribs down.

maintenance.tip: Add a 2-3s squeeze at the top.

hamstring.tip: HAMSTRING ADAPTED: feet a little closer so the glutes lead; stop if the hamstring cramps.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: bridge only to a neutral line, brace the abs.

1.tip: Hips high in an inverted V, lower the head toward the floor, press up. Keep the hips lower or use a wall to regress.

maintenance.tip: Slow descent with a pause.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the hips lower (more incline) and the range shallow; stop before overhead pinch.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: reduce depth.

1.tip: Hold one dumbbell at the chest, squat to parallel, chest tall, drive through the heels.

maintenance.tip: Heaviest controlled load, pause at the bottom.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: reduce depth, knees tracking the toes.

hip.tip: HIP ADAPTED: narrow the stance, control the depth.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the chest up and the core braced; lighten the load.

1.tip: Hinge with a flat back, row the dumbbells to the hips, squeeze the shoulder blades.

maintenance.tip: Heaviest controlled load with a 2s squeeze.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: brace the trunk with a flat back and lighten the load; or support one hand on a bench.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: use a neutral grip and stop before any shoulder pinch.

1.tip: Stand on the band, squat, then drive up and press the handles overhead in one motion.

maintenance.tip: Controlled reps, full squat and press.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: press only to a pain-free height, or squat without the overhead press.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: reduce the squat depth.

1.tip: Anchor the band, sit or stand tall, row the handles to the ribs, squeeze the back.

maintenance.tip: Slower pull with a 2s squeeze at the top.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the elbows low and stop before any shoulder pinch.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: keep the trunk tall and braced; avoid leaning back.

1.tip: Heavy dumbbells or trap bar. Ribs down, shoulders back, walk tall. Grip and trunk in one.

maintenance.tip: Go heavier than Phase 1. Braced trunk, no side lean. Full-body tension carry.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: lighter load, shorter distance. Keep the ribs down and do not let the torso side-bend.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: keep the arms relaxed at the sides — no shrugging. Stop if the shoulder aches.

hip.tip: HIP/PELVIC FLOOR ADAPTED: lighter load. Exhale on the walk, engage the pelvic floor, avoid bearing down.

ankle.tip: ANKLE ADAPTED: shorter, slower steps on a flat surface. Stop if the ankle complains.

1.tip: Chest on an incline bench. Pull elbows past the torso, squeeze 1s. Zero lower-back load.

maintenance.tip: Heavier. Chest stays down on the bench throughout — no cheating with the hips.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: this is the safe row — the bench carries your torso, so the spine is unloaded. Keep the chest down.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: neutral grip, pull to the hip rather than the ribs. Stop before any pinch at the top.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: neutral grip, lighter load. Do not snap into full extension at the bottom.

1.tip: Half-kneeling or standing. Press along the bar's arc — a shoulder-friendly angle, not straight overhead.

maintenance.tip: Heavier. Brace the trunk, press through the arc. Each side.

shoulder.tip: SHOULDER ADAPTED: the landmine arc is the shoulder-friendly press. Keep the hand below eye level and stop before any pinch.

elbow.tip: ELBOW ADAPTED: lighter load, no hard lockout at the top.

lowerBack.tip: LOWER BACK ADAPTED: split stance, ribs down. Squeeze the glute of the rear leg and do not arch to finish the press. Kneel only if the knees are comfortable.

knee.tip: KNEE ADAPTED: press from a STANDING split stance — do not kneel. Keep the front knee soft and behind the toes.
